mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS signals broad job creation, stronger household income, and lower near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month PAYEMS changes do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative changes over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply over three months, labor-market deterioration raises default and earnings-risk expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL combined with a rising FEDFUNDS path signals inflation pressure and policy tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS is above its 3-month moving average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-trend inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-and-tightening signal has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or a Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, leading investors to mark down cyclical earnings over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or no underperformance versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding real output and operating leverage for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO crossings do not have higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative crossings in at least 55% of rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth that exceeds payroll growth signals rising nominal consumption per worker and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and exceeds PAYEMS 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal fails to produce a positive mean 5-day S&P 500 return or if adding PAYEMS to RSAFS does not improve out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp CPIAUCSL increase without a compensating rise in FEDFUNDS lowers ex-ante real policy restraint, raises inflation uncertainty, and pressures equity multiples over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS changes by less than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: inflation scare with delayed monetary response
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-scare episodes deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or show no valuation-sensitive underperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth together with rising RSAFS signals broad real demand and consumption momentum, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS signals have a 60-day S&P 500 hit rate below 50% or underperform the unconditional 60-day return mean.
